_source: Hand-curated from internal/gateway/connector hook profiles plus cli/defenseclaw/inventory/hook_contracts.json. Re-extract on every connector or hook-contract change.
_order: By adoption / popularity — mirrors the hero rotation (lib/hero-connectors.ts) and the docs nav (content/docs/connectors/meta.json).

connectors.label: Claude Code
connectors.hilt: Claude Code supports native PreToolUse ask prompts. CRITICAL findings still block; HIGH findings can pause for approval.
connectors.notes: Hook-only connector — no proxy data path. Observability and PreToolUse deny decisions both flow through the hook bus.

connectors.label: Codex
connectors.hilt: Codex has no native ask surface here; confirm becomes an alert/systemMessage with raw_action preserved. The TUI can review the event but cannot resume it.
connectors.notes: Three telemetry channels at boot: hooks, native OTel, and the notify bridge for agent-turn-complete events. Hook-only connector — no proxy data path.

connectors.label: OpenClaw
connectors.hilt: OpenClaw supports DefenseClaw approval prompts for tool actions. Approvals reach chat-origin sessions via the bundled plugin.
connectors.notes: Reference proxy connector — full data-path interception, sandbox enforcement, plugin-mediated HITL.

connectors.label: Cursor
connectors.hilt: Cursor supports native ask only on documented ask-capable hook events (beforeShellExecution, beforeMCPExecution).
connectors.notes: hooks.json under ~/.cursor; user-global MCP / skills / rules discovery by default, with workspace overlays only when pinned.

connectors.label: Hermes
connectors.hilt: Can block supported hook events but has no native human-approval surface; confirm verdicts fall back explicitly.
connectors.notes: Hooks live in ~/.hermes/config.yaml (hermes-hooks-v1; generic decoder lifts content from the per-event extra envelope). Full shell-hook lifecycle: pre_tool_call blocks, pre_llm_call injects context, post_tool_call/post_llm_call/on_session_start/on_session_end/subagent_stop observe. Setup sets hooks_auto_accept so events register on non-TTY/gateway runs. Discovery surfaces for MCP, skills, plugins.

connectors.label: OpenCode
connectors.hilt: No native human-approval surface; blocks by throwing in the bridge plugin's tool.execute.before. confirm verdicts fall back to allow.
connectors.notes: Governed by a DefenseClaw JS bridge plugin auto-loaded from ~/.config/opencode/plugins/defenseclaw.js (no opencode.json edit). tool.execute.before POSTs to /api/v1/opencode/hook and throws new Error(reason) to abort the tool on a block decision; tool.execute.after is observe-only. Fail-closed is genuine — the bridge throws when the gateway is unreachable and FAIL_MODE=closed.

connectors.label: OmniGent
connectors.hilt: OmniGent parks request, tool_call, and llm_request policy phases for native ASK approval; post-action confirm verdicts use the configured fallback.
connectors.notes: Documented custom Python policy bridge maps all six OmniGent policy phases to DefenseClaw. Optional native OTLP is inactive until the OmniGent launch process exports the standard environment variables; native traces require OmniGent's optional tracing extra.

connectors.label: Gemini CLI
connectors.hilt: Can block supported hook events but has no native human-approval surface; confirm verdicts fall back explicitly.
connectors.notes: settings.json hooks; native OTLP exporter wires directly to the gateway.

connectors.label: GitHub Copilot CLI
connectors.hilt: Copilot CLI supports native ask on documented preToolUse hooks.
connectors.notes: Global hooks under ~/.copilot/hooks by default; optional workspace hooks under <workspace>/.github/hooks.

connectors.label: OpenHands
connectors.hilt: OpenHands has no native ask surface in the documented hook contract; confirm verdicts are downgraded with raw_action preserved and optional additionalContext returned to the agent.
connectors.notes: Global ~/.openhands/hooks.json native hook schema by default; optional repo-local .openhands/hooks.json with --workspace; MCP lives in ~/.openhands/mcp.json; skills include user installs and ~/.openhands/cache/skills/public-skills/skills.

connectors.label: Antigravity
connectors.hilt: Antigravity has an empirically verified native ask on PreToolUse. Returning decision=ask there overrides agy's --dangerously-skip-permissions flag; force_ask is retained only as internal raw_action telemetry.
connectors.notes: DefenseClaw registers the full Antigravity 2.0 lifecycle (PreInvocation, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PostInvocation, Stop) as separate owned keys in ~/.gemini/config/hooks.json (the path agy v1.0.x evaluates). Only PreToolUse is currently advertised as block/ask-capable because it is empirically verified on agy v1.0.1; the other four registrations remain telemetry/spec-parity surfaces until upstream behavior is verified. MCP read/write uses ~/.gemini/config/mcp_config.json and <workspace>/.agents/mcp_config.json. AgentSkills folder form is read/write at ~/.gemini/config/skills and <workspace>/.agents/skills. Plugins install to ~/.gemini/config/plugins or <workspace>/.agents/plugins; the CLI staging path is also discovered. CLI direct markdown skills, rules, and plugin-contained agents remain discovery/scan only.

connectors.label: Windsurf
connectors.hilt: Can block supported hook events but has no native human-approval surface; confirm verdicts fall back explicitly.
connectors.notes: Cascade hooks under ~/.codeium/windsurf/hooks.json. Existing MCP/rules discovered, never auto-created.

connectors.label: ZeptoClaw
connectors.hilt: ZeptoClaw has no native ask surface; confirm uses its explicit fallback with raw_action preserved for TUI/audit review. There is no resumable approval.
connectors.notes: api_base redirect into the DefenseClaw proxy plus response-scan.
